text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE

Patient: Marlena Okonkwo
Referring Service: Breast Surgery / Primary Care
Reason for Consultation: New diagnosis of left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, TNBC, Stage IIB -- evaluation and treatment planning

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Okonkwo is a 38-year-old nulliparous premenopausal woman of West African descent referred for oncology consultation following new diagnosis of triple-negative breast cancer of the left breast. She presents today accompanied by her partner for treatment planning discussion.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo is a 38-year-old woman who first noticed a firmness in her left breast approximately 6 weeks ago while performing self-breast examination. She described it as a hard, non-tender lump in the upper outer area of the left breast. She sought evaluation through her primary care provider, who confirmed a palpable mass and expedited referral to breast surgery. Diagnostic bilateral mammogram demonstrated a 3.1 cm irregular, spiculated, high-density mass in the left upper outer quadrant with associated microcalcifications, BI-RADS 5. Subsequent ultrasound confirmed a 3.1 x 2.8 x 2.5 cm hypoechoic irregular mass at the 10 o'clock position, 3 cm from the nipple. A single enlarged left axillary lymph node (2.0 cm, loss of fatty hilum) was also identified.

Core needle biopsy of the breast mass was performed, confirming invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3 (Nottingham 9/9), ER-negative (Allred 0/8), PR-negative (Allred 0/8), HER2 1+ by IHC (negative). Ki-67 proliferative index 85%. Lymphovascular invasion was present. Stromal TILs noted at 40%. Fine-needle aspiration of the ipsilateral axillary lymph node confirmed metastatic carcinoma consistent with breast primary. PD-L1 CPS was measured at 15 by the 22C3 assay.

Staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast and bone scan showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease. Imaging consistent with locoregional disease only -- no pulmonary nodules, no liver lesions, no osseous lesions on bone scan.

Comprehensive genomic profiling (324-gene panel) on the core biopsy specimen was performed and is notable for: TP53 p.R248W (pathogenic), BRCA1 p.E1250* (pathogenic, VAF 51% -- germline confirmed on reflex testing), PTEN homozygous deletion, RB1 p.Q670*, MYC amplification (copy number 14), CDKN2A p.P114L. Microsatellite stable, TMB 9.2 mut/Mb, HRD score 54 (positive). Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant has been confirmed on reflexed germline testing and reported separately to genetic counseling; patient has been informed.

The case was reviewed at multidisciplinary tumor board today. Consensus: Stage IIB (T2 N1 M0) TNBC with BRCA1 germline mutation; given tumor size, node-positive disease, and aggressive biology, neoadjuvant systemic therapy is the preferred approach to allow for tumor downstaging, assessment of pathologic response, and potential breast-conserving surgery.

Ms. Okonkwo denies fevers, chills, weight loss, night sweats, dyspnea, cough, bone pain, or neurological symptoms. No nipple discharge, skin changes, or palpable right breast lesion. She reports mild anxiety regarding the diagnosis but otherwise feels well and is currently working full-time as a high school biology teacher.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: No unintentional weight loss, no fevers, no drenching night sweats. Mild anxiety and emotional distress since diagnosis.
Pulmonary: No dyspnea at rest or on exertion, no cough, no hemoptysis.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, or changes in bowel habits.
Genitourinary: Regular menstrual cycles, no hematuria.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain, joint pain, or myalgia.
Neurologic: No headaches, focal neurological deficits, or cognitive changes.
Skin: No new rashes or lesions.
Breast: Left breast mass as described. No right breast symptoms.
Endocrine: No heat/cold intolerance, no polyuria or polydipsia.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY

1. Mild intermittent asthma -- well-controlled, no recent exacerbations, no systemic corticosteroid use in past 2 years
2. No prior malignancy
3. No prior breast biopsies or imaging abnormalities
4. No cardiac disease, hypertension, or diabetes

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY

None

GYNECOLOGIC HISTORY

Nulligravida, nulliparous. Regular menstrual cycles, approximately 28-day cycle, LMP approximately 2 weeks prior to this visit. No oral contraceptive use currently (discontinued approximately 5 years ago). No hormone replacement therapy. No history of endometriosis or PCOS.

FAMILY HISTORY

Significant for breast/ovarian cancer on the maternal side:
- Maternal aunt: ovarian cancer, diagnosed age 52
- Maternal grandmother: breast cancer, diagnosed age 60 (laterality unknown to patient)
- No paternal family history of breast, ovarian, prostate, or pancreatic cancer reported
- No known family members with confirmed BRCA1/2 testing prior to patient's diagnosis

SOCIAL HISTORY

Occupation: High school biology teacher, currently full-time
Smoking: Never smoker
Alcohol: Social/occasional, reports approximately 1-2 drinks per week
Recreational drugs: Denies
Marital status: Not married, lives with long-term partner
Children: None (nulliparous)
Exercise: Reports regular walking and occasional yoga prior to diagnosis
Support system: Partner present at visit, described as supportive; patient has close relationship with her mother

ALLERGIES

NKDA (no known drug allergies)

MEDICATIONS

1. Albuterol HFA inhaler 90 mcg/actuation -- 1-2 puffs inhaled PRN shortness of breath or wheezing (uses approximately 1-2 times per month)
2. Multivitamin -- 1 tablet orally daily

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

General: Well-appearing woman, alert and oriented x3, in no acute distress. Mildly anxious. Well-nourished.
Vital Signs: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 76 bpm, RR 16, Temp 98.4°F, SpO2 99% on room air, Height 5'6", Weight 68 kg, BSA 1.77 m²
ECOG Performance Status: 0

HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis. Sclera anicteric.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable masses.
Musculoskeletal: No bony tenderness to palpation over sternum, ribs, or spine.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. No lymphedema.

Breast Examination:
- Left breast: A firm, irregular, approximately 3 cm palpable mass in the upper outer quadrant at approximately 10 o'clock, 3 cm from the nipple. The mass is non-tender but appears somewhat fixed to surrounding parenchymal tissue. No skin dimpling, peau d'orange, or erythema. Nipple intact, no discharge expressed. A single firm, discrete left axillary lymph node approximately 2 cm is palpable -- mildly mobile, non-tender.
- Right breast: No palpable masses, asymmetry, skin changes, or nipple abnormality. Right axilla: no palpable lymphadenopathy.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 upper and lower extremities. Sensation intact. No focal deficits.

---

RELEVANT LABORATORY AND DIAGNOSTIC DATA

CBC: WBC 6.8 x10^9/L (differential normal), Hemoglobin 12.8 g/dL, Platelets 228 x10^9/L
CMP: Na 139, K 4.0, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 11, Creatinine 0.8, Glucose 89, Total bilirubin 0.6, ALT 22 U/L, AST 18 U/L, Alk Phos 62 U/L, Albumin 4.2 g/dL
LFTs within normal limits -- relevant given planned anthracycline therapy
ECHOCARDIOGRAM (obtained prior to anthracycline): LVEF 63%, no significant wall motion abnormality, no pericardial effusion -- adequate cardiac function for doxorubicin

Biopsy/Pathology (reviewed in detail):
- Core needle biopsy left breast UOQ: IDC, grade 3, Nottingham 9/9, ER 0%, PR 0%, HER2 1+ (IHC), Ki-67 85%, LVI present, sTILs 40%, PD-L1 CPS 15
- Left axillary LN FNA: metastatic carcinoma, breast primary

NGS (comprehensive genomic profiling, primary tumor):
- Pathogenic: TP53 p.R248W, BRCA1 p.E1250* (germline confirmed), PTEN deletion, RB1 p.Q670*
- MYC amplification CN14, CDKN2A p.P114L
- MSS, TMB 9.2 mut/Mb, HRD 54 (positive), gLOH 22%
- No actionable fusions

Imaging:
- Mammogram/US: 3.1 cm left UOQ mass, BI-RADS 5; 2.0 cm left axillary node
- CT C/A/P + bone scan: No distant metastatic disease, consistent with locoregional Stage IIB

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

38-year-old premenopausal woman with newly diagnosed Stage IIB (T2 N1 M0) invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, grade 3, triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-), Ki-67 85%, with confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (p.E1250*) and HRD-positive tumor. PD-L1 CPS 15 on the primary tumor.

1. NEOADJUVANT SYSTEMIC THERAPY -- PLANNED

Following multidisciplinary tumor board review, we will pursue neoadjuvant therapy rather than upfront surgery. Rationale: (a) T2N1 disease, node-positive TNBC, high-grade, high Ki-67 -- strong indication for systemic therapy; (b) neoadjuvant approach enables pathologic response assessment and may allow breast-conserving surgery; (c) PD-L1 CPS 15 makes this patient an excellent candidate for pembrolizumab-based neoadjuvant therapy per KEYNOTE-522 data.

Plan: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV day 1 of each 21-day cycle, combined with:
  - Phase 1 (approximately 8 weeks): Dose-dense AC -- doxorubicin (Adriamycin) 60 mg/m² IV + cyclophosphamide 600 mg/m² IV every 14 days x 4 cycles (ddAC x4). Pegfilgrastim 6 mg SC on day 2 of each ddAC cycle for primary febrile neutropenia prophylaxis. Pembrolizumab cycles 1 and 3 co-administered with ddAC cycles 1 and 3 (given 3-week pembro cycle and 2-week AC cycle, timing will be coordinated by infusion nursing per schedule).
  - Phase 2 (approximately 12 weeks): Paclitaxel (Taxol) 80 mg/m² IV weekly x 12 weeks with pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks concurrently.

Antiemetic prophylaxis for ddAC: ondansetron 8 mg IV, dexamethasone 12 mg IV, aprepitant 125 mg PO day 1 (then 80 mg PO days 2-3). Recommended olanzapine 10 mg PO QHS days 1-4 as well given high emetogenic risk. Pre-meds for paclitaxel: diphenhydramine 25 mg IV, famotidine 20 mg IV, dexamethasone 20 mg IV.

Baseline ECHO obtained -- LVEF 63%, adequate.
Baseline LFTs normal.
Patient counseled on pembro toxicities including irAE monitoring (thyroid, liver, lung, skin, adrenal, pituitary). Immunotherapy toxicity handout provided.

2. GERMLINE BRCA1 MUTATION

Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant p.E1250* confirmed. Referral placed to certified genetic counselor for formal pre/post-test counseling, family communication letter, and surveillance recommendations. Implications discussed with patient:
- Elevated lifetime risk for contralateral breast cancer (~40-60% cumulative risk)
- Elevated lifetime risk for ovarian cancer (~35-46% cumulative risk with BRCA1)
- Risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) recommended generally between ages 35-40 for BRCA1 carriers -- will revisit after completion of neoadjuvant treatment and surgery
- First-degree relatives (sisters, mother) should be offered cascade testing
- Implications for PARP inhibitor eligibility (olaparib) if needed in the future

3. FERTILITY PRESERVATION COUNSELING

Patient is nulliparous and premenopausal. The urgency of initiating chemotherapy and the patient's own time constraints were discussed in detail. Options reviewed: oocyte cryopreservation (egg freezing), embryo cryopreservation. Referral offered to reproductive endocrinology; patient declined at this time citing time constraints and the desire to start treatment promptly. This decision was documented and respected. Patient counseled that chemotherapy-induced premature ovarian insufficiency is a risk with this regimen. We will monitor FSH/LH/estradiol and AMH pre- and post-treatment. Patient was provided with written information and may revisit fertility options if she changes her mind.

4. LABS AND MONITORING

Pre-treatment baseline: CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH, free T4, pregnancy test (urine hCG -- patient advised to use non-hormonal contraception during treatment), hepatitis B sAg and sAb (HBSAB) prior to pembrolizumab initiation, ECHO (completed).
Monitoring during treatment: CBC and CMP with each ddAC cycle. LFTs, TSH, free T4 each pembrolizumab cycle. EKG not required at baseline given normal ECHO.

5. SUPPORTIVE CARE

- Social work referral placed (psychosocial support, financial assistance resources)
- Oncology dietitian referral offered
- Patient given Infusion center orientation appointment
- Discussed alopecia as expected side effect with doxorubicin-based therapy; cold cap therapy discussed but not offered with this regimen given tumor biology and concern for scalp metastasis prophylaxis
- Patient encouraged to remain active as tolerated

6. PLAN / FOLLOW-UP

- Start pembrolizumab + ddAC cycle 1 within the next 7-10 days once pre-treatment work-up complete
- Follow up in clinic on day 1 of each cycle for toxicity assessment
- Mid-treatment response assessment MRI of left breast/axilla planned after completion of ddAC x4 (prior to starting weekly Taxol phase)
- Surgical oncology to remain engaged for post-neoadjuvant surgical planning

Extensive time was spent with Ms. Okonkwo and her partner today -- approximately 75 minutes. All questions addressed. She understands the diagnosis, treatment rationale, and goals of therapy. She is motivated and engaged in her care.

Signed,
Attending Oncologist, Hematology/Oncology
[Dictated and electronically signed]
